title: Fixing CSS Inconsistent Grid Layout Column Widths with Variable Items

statement.markdown: Given a CSS grid layout with variable item counts, write a function that calculates the minimum column width required to accommodate all items. The function should take an object representing the grid layout and return the minimum column width.
statement.inputFormat: Grid layout object with item counts and widths
statement.notes: The grid layout object has the following structure: { 'rows': 2, 'cols': 3, 'items': [{ 'width': 100, 'height': 200 }, { 'width': 150, 'height': 300 }, { 'width': 200, 'height': 400 }] }

examples.explanation: The minimum column width required is 400 to accommodate all items
The minimum column width required is 400 to accommodate all items

hints.text: Calculate the maximum item width to determine the minimum column width

editorial.title: Editorial: Calculating Minimum Column Width
editorial.intuition: The minimum column width required is the maximum item width to accommodate all items
editorial.betterSolution: Use a single pass through the grid items to calculate the maximum item width
editorial.optimalSolution: Iterate through the grid items, keep track of the maximum item width, and return it as the minimum column width
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The optimal solution has a time complexity of O(n) where n is the number of grid items
editorial.dryRun: Test the function with a small grid layout to ensure it produces the correct minimum column width
commonMistakes: Failing to handle variable item counts
Not considering the maximum item width

functionSignature.python:
def calculate_min_column_width(self, layout) -> int:
  return max_item_width
